page_content:
ECON677 International Trade and Applications (3 Credits)

A mix of theory and empirical work. Students will analyze the causes and consequences of international trade. The course will cover a set of conceptual tools that are useful for understanding "globalization" and the usage of these tools to address interesting and important questions about how countries, firms, and workers respond to international trade. Prerequisite: ECON641 and ECON644.

Restriction: Must be in MS Applied Economics program; or with permission from the program director.
